name: use_case_generation
label: Use Case Generation
purpose: Generate business use cases from requirements and lifecycle context, then normalize output to repository standards.
when_to_use: User asks to generate initial use cases from BA baseline
Requirements exist but BA/use-cases are incomplete
Need to refresh use-case docs after baseline changes
methodology: Read requirements baseline and identify core user/system goals, actors, and workflow steps.
Follow methodology/ba-methods.md sections 9 and 10 for use case definition and derivation discipline.
Apply methodology/ba-methods.md section 10.2A Use Case Granularity Decision Rule before finalizing use case boundaries.
Draft concise use cases with consistent numbering and naming.
Ensure each use case includes: Description, Actors, Preconditions, Main Flow, Postconditions.
Prefer incremental creation; keep existing files unless overwrite is explicitly requested.
After AI drafting, run tool normalization/write flow to persist consistent markdown.

tooling.fallback_behavior: When AI is unavailable, tool generates deterministic baseline use cases.

completion_criteria: At least one use case is generated when baseline supports it.
All generated use cases contain required sections.
Project state is updated with generated artifacts.
constraints: Do not invent domain-critical constraints not present in baseline.
Do not create standalone use cases for atomic actions (submit, cancel, approve, reject, update, save, notify) unless they satisfy the standalone criteria in methodology/ba-methods.md section 10.2A.
When standalone criteria are not met, model such actions as main, alternate, or exception flows inside a broader actor-goal use case.
Do not embed implementation design (specific APIs, DB tables/columns, frameworks, or infrastructure products) in use-case steps unless explicitly required by source policy or regulation.
Keep use cases goal-oriented and actor-centric, describing what must happen and expected outcomes rather than how software is technically implemented.
Do not overwrite existing use-case files unless force/explicit overwrite is requested.
Do not emit partial documents missing required sections.
